findings.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
findings.what: 0 of 0 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
findings.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

findings.effort: Often one default setting in your export.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
findings.what: 788 of 788 trips don't say whether the vehicle is wheelchair accessible.
findings.why: Even with accessible stops, riders need to know the bus itself can take them.

findings.effort: A small file for most flat-fare systems.
findings.fix: Add fare_attributes.txt (or Fares v2 files) with your fare structure. If your service is fare-free, ask to have it marked fare-free instead.
findings.what: The feed contains no fare information.
findings.why: Riders see 'fare unknown' in trip planners and can't budget their trip; visitors are most affected.

findings.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Rename stops to mixed case (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave').
findings.what: About 0 stop names are written in ALL CAPS.
findings.why: Mixed-case names are easier to read in apps and are read more naturally by screen readers.

findings.effort: Usually a bulk edit in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Populate trip_headsign to match what the bus displays.
findings.why: Riders at the stop can't tell which direction a bus is going.

findings.effort: A small file; one rule often covers the whole service.
findings.fix: Add booking_rules.txt saying how far ahead and how to book (phone, app, or web).
findings.what: This feed describes flexible (demand-responsive) service but has no booking_rules.txt.
findings.why: Riders can see the service area but not how or when to reserve a trip, so they can't actually use the service.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 0% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (0% marked accessible, 0% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is not published.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'empty_file' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Empty file (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'invalid_phone_number' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Invalid phone number (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: One setting in most export tools.
findings.fix: Remove past service periods the next time you export the feed.
findings.what: Some service calendars in the feed have already ended.
findings.why: Expired calendars are dead weight and can hide real schedule problems from your staff and vendors.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'service_window_outside_feed_period' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Service window outside feed period (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'big_gap_in_service' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Big gap in service (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 5 kinds of issue across 11 instances (2 error, 3 warning, 6 informational).

findings.fix: Re-export with a validity window that reaches at least 60 days out.
findings.what: Service data runs out in 29 day(s).
findings.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency. Fixing it now is calmer than after riders notice.

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data runs out in 29 day(s). Publish an updated feed soon or riders will lose trip planning.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Sampled 9 times outside service hours: 3 of 3 feeds healthy.

criteria.detail: Service data runs out in 30 days; renew to qualify.
States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

conformance.summary: This feed is close to the conformance mark. 2 validator errors to resolve. Service data runs out in 30 days; renew to qualify. States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

feed.license_note: No stated data license in the Mobility Database; verify before publishing.

fetch.user_agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/125.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

ntd_id_alignment.detail: Setting your GTFS agency_id to your five-digit NTD ID is an optional way to line a feed up with its National Transit Database record. FTA links the two on your P-50 form, so it is not a required feed change. We don't have your NTD ID on file, so this is not checked yet.

pillars.detail: Service data runs out in 30 days; renew before you certify.

ntd_readiness.summary: This feed is close to NTD-ready. 2 validator errors to resolve. Service data runs out in 30 days; renew before you certify.

recommendations.effort: A small file; one rule often covers the whole service.
recommendations.fix: Add booking_rules.txt with how far ahead and how to reserve (phone, app, or web).
recommendations.what: Flex zones are published but no booking_rules.txt is present.
recommendations.why: Riders can see the service area but not how to book, so they can't complete a trip.

recommendations.effort: A per-route color setting in your scheduling software.
recommendations.fix: Adjust route_color or route_text_color so each pair clears 4.5:1. Often switching the text between black and white is enough.
recommendations.what: 1 route badge(s) pair a color and text color below the WCAG 4.5:1 contrast bar (GC).
recommendations.why: Low-contrast route badges are hard to read for riders with low vision and wash out on a phone screen in sunlight at the stop.

top_fixes.fix: Re-export with a validity window that reaches at least 60 days out.
top_fixes.what: Service data runs out in 29 day(s).
top_fixes.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency. Fixing it now is calmer than after riders notice.

top_fixes.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'empty_file' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
top_fixes.what: Empty file (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
top_fixes.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

top_fixes.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'invalid_phone_number' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
top_fixes.what: Invalid phone number (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
top_fixes.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.
